# FM cockpit state-dot, pulse and health primitives (reduced-motion honored)

## Context

T1.4 design-system leaf of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — the smallest visual primitives every fleet surface composes: the agent state dot (with the live-pulse animation), the family rail, and the health-summary swatches. Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561); consumes the token layer (#14578).

## The Problem

The SSOT artifact defines these as raw CSS (`.dot` + `@keyframes pulse` gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion`, `.agent::before` family rail, `.sw` swatches). Without a component-level primitive, every view leaf re-implements them — the exact per-view accretion the epic exists to kill — and the anti-lock-in render rules (ADR 0032 §2.3) would be re-derived per consumer instead of enforced once.

## The Architectural Reality

- Neo functional/component primitives under the target namespace (#14577 gate decides placement); these are leaf components/mixins consuming #14578 tokens only — zero hand-rolled colors.
- ADR 0032 bindings enforced HERE once: the family rail binds an **episode attribute** (data-driven, never a per-agent constant — a family switch re-renders the rail, same resident); state encodes SESSION state (working/idle/wedged/limited/benched — never identity).
- `prefers-reduced-motion` gating is carried from the artifact (pulse is decoration, not information — the dot's color carries the signal).

## The Fix

One PR, the per-view triplet: (1) the primitives (StateDot w/ optional pulse, FamilyRail binding, HealthSwatch) as reusable components/mixins; (2) unit specs (state→token mapping, episode-attribute binding, reduced-motion config path); (3) a minimal NL-verifiable render surface (the primitives mounted in the dev-server, inspectable via Neural Link).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] StateDot renders all five states from #14578 tokens; pulse only when live AND motion allowed (`prefers-reduced-motion` honored via the artifact's gating).
- [ ] FamilyRail binds a data field (episode attribute), not a constant; changing the bound value re-renders in place (ADR 0032 §2.3.3 fixture).
- [ ] HealthSwatch set renders the five categories; zero hand-rolled color values anywhere (tokens only — lint-greppable).
- [ ] Unit specs cover the three primitives' config→render mapping (custom playwright config, never default `npx playwright test`).
- [ ] NL-verifiable render surface exists (wire-binding slice of the triplet).

## Out of Scope

- The AgentCard composition (T2.7 consumes these) · the health summary BAR layout (T2.8) · token values (#14578).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · #14578 (consumed, blocked-by) · #14577 (namespace gate) · ADR 0032 §2.3 (render rules enforced at this layer) · D#14561.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-04T03:30:30Z

**Scope narrowed (horizon map v2, B-split — author's own re-shape):** this leaf is now **StateDot only** (dot + pulse + reduced-motion gating). The two siblings are their own one-PR leaves: **#14635** (FamilyRail — the §2.3.3 rebind-in-place primitive) and **#14637** (HealthSwatch — the five-category unit). All three are CLAIMABLE now (the token floor merged at `ee5f92b63`, relocating to `apps/agentos/resources/tokens.css` via PR #14629); placement = the agentos FM module per the `#14577` veto. ACs here apply to the StateDot scope only. 🖖 Vega

Placement: `apps/agentos/view/fleet/` per the `#14642` convention. 🖖 Vega

- 2026-07-04T05:24:32Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `8be3511` - "refactor(agentos): narrow to StateDot-only per review — split siblings to #14635/#14637 (#14593)

GPT's review (PR #14700) correctly flagged a scope-ledger mismatch: #14593
was narrowed to StateDot-only, and FamilyRail (#14635) + HealthSwatch (#14637)
are open sibling leaves with STRICTER ACs than the versions I bundled here
satisfied — #14635 wants unknown-family → neutral + an 'unclassified' badge
(mine fell back to human), #14637 wants unknown-category → literal text + a
count slot for the #14599 bar (mine had neither).

So this PR narrows to StateDot only (its true #14593 scope): removes the
under-built FamilyRail.mjs + HealthSwatch.mjs and their CSS/tests. FamilyRail
and HealthSwatch will be built properly under #14635/#14637 against their full
ACs. 3 StateDot unit tests green (state→token mapping, live→fm-live reduced-
motion path, unknown→off fallback)."
- 2026-07-04T07:13:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `1bc0708` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit StateDot session-state primitive (#14593) (#14700)

* feat(agentos): fleet cockpit state primitives — StateDot, FamilyRail, HealthSwatch (#14593)

The smallest visual primitives every fleet surface composes, built as Neo
functional components under apps/agentos/view/fleet/, consuming the #14578
--fm-* token layer (zero hand-rolled colors). The anti-lock-in render rules
are enforced once, here, instead of re-derived per consumer:

- StateDot: session state (ok/idle/wedged/limited/off) maps to --fm-state-*
  via a pure, exported stateToken() SSOT; a live config adds fm-live, whose
  pulse is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion in fleet-components.css (color
  carries the signal, motion is decoration). State is never identity.
- FamilyRail: family maps to --fm-family-* bound data-driven from the current
  episode's family key — a family swap re-renders the SAME resident.
- HealthSwatch: the health-summary legend entry, reusing stateToken as the
  one source of truth for state color.

fleet-components.css carries geometry + the reduced-motion-gated keyframes;
index.html links it beside tokens.css. 7 unit tests green: state/family/label
mappings, the live->fm-live config path, unknown->off fallback, and the
family-swap-same-resident id-stability.

* refactor(agentos): narrow to StateDot-only per review — split siblings to #14635/#14637 (#14593)

motion path, unknown→off fallback)."
